text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 24 April 2018

 Dr Kate 
 Health Centre 

 Keele University, Keele, Newcastle

 Staffordshire ST5 5BG 
Dear  Dr Kate 
 Adam Harris , DOB:  12-03-1986 , Hospital No:  A913527  NHS No.  7719035462 
 12 Firs Crescent, Newcastle-under-Lyme, ST5 1LP 

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent seizures of uncertain classification; previous EEGs without definitive lateralising or focal features; MRI brain (2015) structurally unremarkable. Implanted vagus nerve stimulator (VNS) in 2016; no parameter changes at this review.

Previous medication: Sodium valproate (sedation, weight gain), carbamazepine (dizziness, hyponatraemia), levetiracetam (irritability), lamotrigine (partial response), clobazam PRN (effective for clusters but daytime sleepiness), lacosamide (no clear benefit), topiramate (cognitive slowing), zonisamide (appetite suppression). 

Not taken: perampanel, brivaracetam, eslicarbazepine, pregabalin, tiagabine, rufinamide.

I reviewed  Adam Harris  in clinic today, who attended with his partner. He reported variable energy levels but is working full-time and driving is not undertaken. Sleep has been irregular due to shift patterns, and he has been keeping a seizure diary. In Nov he presented with a cluster of three seizures (self-terminating, fluctuating awareness). In Feb a nocturnal seizure were noted, and in Apr a single generalised tonic-clonic seizure occurred (not witnessed by family). There have been no clear precipitating infections; he describes stress and sleep loss as possible triggers. No injuries, tongue biting reported once in February, and post-ictal confusion lasted 20–30 minutes after the April event. 

VNS: Implanted left-sided, generator functioning. Interrogation today showed normal lead impedance and battery within expected range. No parameter changes at this review. He uses the magnet occasionally; he feels it shortens post-ictal recovery but has not aborted events entirely. No cough, voice change, or dysphagia beyond baseline.

Current medication: 
- Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily
- Clobazam 10 mg at night PRN during clusters (used on two occasions since November)
- Folic acid 5 mg daily

Investigations: Routine bloods (U&E, LFT, FBC) February 2018 satisfactory; sodium 137 mmol/L, ALT 22 U/L. ECG 2017 normal sinus rhythm. No new imaging requested today. 

Plan:
- Continue current lamotrigine dose; clobazam PRN for clusters may be continued as currently used.
- Reinforce sleep hygiene and regular routines; advised to avoid shift pattern changes where possible.
- Safety counselling updated: supervision near water, avoidance of heights, first aid advice reviewed with partner; not driving and aware of DVLA regulations.
- Seizure diary to be maintained; partner to record nocturnal events with brief descriptors and approximate durations.
- VNS: no changes today; next interrogation at routine follow-up unless issues arise.
- Consideration of perampanel or brivaracetam at future review if events remain frequent; patient prefers to defer additional medication for now.

Follow-up: Nurse telephone review in 6–8 weeks to check frequency and tolerability; routine clinic in 4–6 months or earlier if further clusters occur.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: In Nov he presented with a cluster of three seizures... In Feb a nocturnal seizure were noted, and in Apr a single generalised tonic-clonic seizure occurred

In Nov he presented with a cluster of three seizures (self-terminating, fluctuating awareness). In Feb a nocturnal seizure were noted, and in Apr a single generalised tonic-clonic seizure occurred (not witnessed by family)

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter provides specific events within recent months: "In Nov he presented with a cluster of three seizures" (3 seizures), "In Feb a nocturnal seizure were noted" (1 seizure), and "in Apr a single generalised tonic-clonic seizure occurred" (1 seizure). These are three distinct months within a roughly 6‑month span leading up to the clinic date (24 April 2018). Total seizures mentioned: 3 (Nov) + 1 (Feb) + 1 (Apr) = 5 seizures across approximately 6 months. No statement of longer seizure-free period. Therefore, normalize to 5 per 6 months.
